script.text: Namaste. Welcome to this guided meditation for temple visit preparation.
Find a quiet space where you won't be disturbed for the next 10 minutes.
Sit in a comfortable position with your spine straight. You may sit cross-legged on the floor or in a chair with feet flat on the ground.
Gently close your eyes, or lower your gaze to the ground.
Take a moment to arrive here, letting go of any distractions or worries.
When you're ready, we'll begin with conscious breathing.
Now, bring your attention to your natural breath. Don't try to change it, just observe.
Notice the cool air entering through your nostrils, and the warm air leaving.
Let's practice a sacred breathing pattern. Inhale slowly through your nose for a count of four.
Hold gently for four counts.
Exhale slowly through your nose for four counts.
And hold empty for four counts.
Continue this pattern for the next few breaths on your own. I'll remain silent.
Beautiful. Now return to your natural breathing rhythm.
With your mind calm and body relaxed, let's prepare your spirit for the sacred temple visit.
Visualize yourself standing at the entrance of the temple. See the ancient architecture, feel the spiritual energy.
As you imagine stepping inside, feel a wave of peace washing over you.
This temple is not just a physical place, but a sacred space within your own heart.
Silently, offer a prayer of gratitude. Thank the divine for this opportunity to connect.
Feel your heart opening, becoming receptive to spiritual blessings.
If any mantras come to mind, you may silently chant them now.
Know that you carry this sacred space within you, wherever you go.
You are now prepared, both mentally and spiritually, for your temple visit.
Begin to bring gentle movement back to your fingers and toes.
Take a deep, refreshing breath in, and sigh it out.
When you're ready, slowly open your eyes.
May your temple visit be filled with peace, devotion, and spiritual awakening. Namaste.
